chain_edges.notes: 5-pole hyperscaler-lab exclusive alliance regime feeds directly into the compute-stack lock-in claim; focal extends af6de8 with funding-origin serving topology.
3-cloud frontier-training capacity capture is the structural precondition that makes funding-origin lock-in inevitable for the focal.
Compute-stack lock-in by funding origin reinforces dual-track training/inference SKU adoption across MAIA/MTIA hyperscaler silicon.
SGLang CVE-2026-5760 GGUF SSTI is one of the four Apr 19-26 inference-runtime CVEs the focal prediction names as composing the AI-Infra category.
GGUF-to-RCE-on-SGLang reporting reinforces inference-server template/loader RCE as the canonical AI-Infra CVE pattern.
The Apr 21 Comment-and-Control chain across Claude Code / Gemini CLI / Copilot is one of four anchor incidents in the focal's inference-runtime + agentic-CI composite.
An AI-Infra CVE class with NVD tagging would land CVSS 8.0+ LLM-integration entries on Patch Tuesday rollups, the recurring pattern downstream forecasts.
27B Dense on RTX 4090 with flagship coding quality is the technical substrate that makes consumer-GPU coding agents displace cloud APIs.
Individual/SMB local-first inversion scales up into the enterprise hybrid harness claim of the focal prediction.
1M context as open-weight floor (DeepSeek V4, Qwen3.6) underwrites the local-side capability needed for hybrid harness coding workflows.

relations.notes: Focal is a stronger restatement of af6de8's 5-pole alliance regime; compute-stack lock-in implies the exclusive alliance shape.
Both describe hyperscaler-lab capital concentration; 81d3055 focuses on training-capacity oligopoly, focal on funding-paid-in-compute lock-in.
Both describe the Q3 2026 closed-frontier regime, but along different axes (open vs closed weights vs hyperscaler-bound serving).
Both reference Claude Opus 4.7 and GPT-5.5 as anchors for Q3 2026 frontier landscape, but on context-length vs serving-topology axes.
GGUF supply-chain hardening is a narrower instance of the focal's inference-server template/loader RCE category; the focal subsumes it.
CI-agent secret exfiltration and inference-server CVE class share the agentic-CI surface but target distinct CWE buckets and standards bodies.
Indirect prompt injection as recurring CVE category and AI-Infra CVE tagging are complementary axes inside the LLM Top 10 v2026 reshuffle.
MCP-protocol attack surface and inference-server runtime form sibling new categories under OWASP LLM Top 10 v2026 but cover non-overlapping layers.
Third-party preview-vendor boundary hardening targets access-control layer; focal targets runtime CWE schema. Independent facets of the same Q3 2026 hardening wave.
The focal hybrid-harness claim presupposes the 27B Dense + 1M context standard becoming the on-prem default.
Both predict local-first defaulting; focal targets enterprise IDEs while 4bd33d targets individual / SMB stacks.
Both formalize the local-for-utility / cloud-for-frontier split; focal frames it as IDE hybrid harness, b60e42 as open-vs-closed weights.
Both rest on the open-weight Qwen3.6 / DeepSeek V4 wave but address different layers (IDE harness vs context-floor / RAG flip).
Both push edge-LLM viability; 4bd551 from 1-bit native training, focal from 27B Dense + Apple Silicon throughput.
